# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { VercelRequest, VercelResponse } from '@vercel/node'  // Import the necessary types for handling requests and responses in a serverless function using NodeJS environment (Vercel Edge Functions)
const defaultConfig = process.env.EDGE_CONFIG ? JSON.parse(process.env.EDGE_CONFIG): { API_KEY: 'vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL:'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com' }; // Parse the config if it exists, otherwise use default values
const apiKey = process.env.API_KEY || defaultConfig.API_KEY;  // Use API key from environment variables or defaults to configuration file (if not set)
// Assuming DB URL is in a format like 'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com' and we are assuming that the user, pass & db name will be present as well if they exist otherwise it should use default values from config 
const [protocol, authInfo, hostname] = (defaultConfig.DB_URL || "").split('//')[1].split(':'); // Split DB URL into protocol and authentication info to get user & pass part of the url; assumes dbUrl is in format 'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com'
const [username, password] = authInfo ? authInfo.split('@')[1].split(':'): ['','']; // Split username and password from authentication info to get user & pass part of the url; assumes dbUrl is in format 'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com'
const database = (defaultConfig.DB_URL || "").split('//')[1].split('/')[2]; // Assuming DB URL has a specific structure, split it into protocol and the rest to get db name part of url; assumes that there is only one '/', then we are assuming first word after ':' as database
const [port] = (defaultConfig.DB_URL || "").split(':')[1].split('/'); // Assuming DB URL has a specific structure, split it into port and the rest to get db name part of url; assumes that there is only one ':', then we are assuming first word after ':' as database
const parsedUrl = `${protocol}://${username}:${password}@${hostname}:${port}/${database}?sslmode=require` // Construct the DB URL with all parts filled in. 
// Assuming that we are using SSL mode require, if not then it will be empty string '' or 'disable' depending on environment variable set for ssl_required (default is false) and dbUrl has a specific structure to construct database url; assumes there should only one question mark after DB URL construction. 
const featureFlag = process.env.FEATUREFLAG || defaultConfig.featureflag // Use Feature flag from Environment Variables or defaults if not set in Edge Config file (if it exists)  
export const config =  { API_KEY, DB_URL: parsedUrl , FEATUREFLAG : `${process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' ? featureFlag  : defaultConfig.featureflag}` }; // Store all the configuration details into a single object for easy access and usage in your code
export const handler = (req: VercelRequest, res: VercelResponse) => {  
    if(process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'){  // If running on production environment then use feature flag else default config file details to serve the request as per requirement in your code; assumes that NODE_ENV is set and it should be either development or Production (default) depending upon where you run this function
        res.json({config: {API_KEY, DB_URL : parsedUrl , FEATUREFLAG}});  // Send response with all the configuration details in JSON format to client as per requirement; assumes that NODE_ENV is set and it should be either development or Production (default) depending upon where you run this function
    } else {   res.json({config: defaultConfig}); };// If running on local environment then use defaults config file detailsto serve the request to client as per requirement in your code; assumes that NODE_ENV is not set and it should be either development or Production (default) depending upon where you run this function
};  // End of handler definition. This will handle all incoming requests from clients, based on configuration details stored at runtime for fast global access
